rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when adding annex content; modify only the owning target declaration.
Keep type-scope content and implementation-scope content separated; do not move architecture elements across scopes.
Do not place references to implementation-only paths or subcomponent ports into a broader owner scope where they are not visible.
generic_rules: Attach new content only to the intended owner declaration and leave unrelated declarations unchanged.
Use only identifiers that are visible in the chosen owner scope.
Prefer the minimal insertion that preserves surrounding structure and formatting.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Implementation-level bindings belong in implementation scopes after relevant structural declarations.
Component-type contracts may reference only features visible at that type scope, not internal subcomponent paths.
anti_patterns: Do not insert content into the wrong owner or duplicate annex blocks across both type and implementation without need.
Do not alter connections, subcomponents, features, or properties when the task is only to add annex content.
irrelevant_topics: Specific contract clause forms and expression patterns are downstream generation concerns, not fusion concerns.
Validator-driven type fixes are not relevant when no diagnostics are active.
